---
name: zigmodu-module
description: Create a new ZigModu module with best-practice structure. Use when adding a new domain module, CRUD resource, or business logic unit to a ZigModu project.
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob
---

# Create a ZigModu Module

## ⛔ CRITICAL: Read Before Acting

**Generated files (marked `//! ⛔ @generated by zmodu`) are read-only.** AI writes ONLY in `ext/` subdirectory. If you need to change generated behavior, update schema.sql and re-run `zmodu scaffold`.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Generate from SQL (preferred)
zmodu orm --sql schema.sql --out src/modules

# Create empty module skeleton
zmodu module <name>
```

## Module Structure (5 files + ext/)

```
src/modules/<name>/
├── module.zig       # ⛔ lifecycle + barrel re-exports + health checks
├── model.zig        # ⛔ data structs + sql_table_name + json_names
├── persistence.zig  # ⛔ ORM Repository(T) accessors
├── service.zig      # ⛔ CRUD + validate + EventBus
├── api.zig          # ⛔ registerRoutes + handlers
└── ext/             # ✅ AI writes here
    ├── service.zig  # custom business methods
    └── api.zig      # custom API endpoints
```

## Manual Module Creation Checklist

When creating a module by hand (no SQL schema):

### 1. module.zig — Lifecycle + Barrel + Health
```zig
//! Module: <name>
const std = @import("std");
const zigmodu = @import("zigmodu");

pub const info = zigmodu.api.Module{
    .name = "<name>",
    .description = "<name> module",
    .dependencies = &.{},
    .is_internal = false,
};

pub fn init() !void {
    std.log.info("[{s}] initializing", .{info.name});
}
pub fn deinit() void {
    std.log.info("[{s}] shutting down", .{info.name});
}
pub fn registerHealthChecks(endpoint: *zigmodu.HealthEndpoint) !void {
    try endpoint.registerCheck("<name>", "<name> health", zigmodu.HealthEndpoint.alwaysUp);
}

// Barrel re-exports — placed at bottom of module.zig
pub const model = @import("model.zig");
pub const persistence = @import("persistence.zig");
pub const service = @import("service.zig");
pub const api = @import("api.zig");
```

### 2. model.zig — Data Structures
```zig
pub const Thing = struct {
    pub const sql_table_name: []const u8 = "thing";
    id: ?i64 = null,         // PK: always optional with = null
    name: []const u8,        // NOT NULL, no default → required
    email: ?[]const u8 = null, // nullable or has default → optional
};
```
Rules:
- Primary key: `?i64 = null` (auto-generated)
- NOT NULL without default: `Type` (required)
- Nullable or has DEFAULT: `?Type = null` (optional)
- `= null` is mandatory for Zig 0.16 `parseFromSlice` compatibility

### 3. persistence.zig — ORM Repository
```zig
const data = @import("zigmodu").data;
const model = @import("model.zig");

pub const ThingPersistence = struct {
    backend: data.SqlxBackend,
    orm: data.orm.Orm(data.SqlxBackend),

    pub fn init(backend: data.SqlxBackend) ThingPersistence {
        return .{ .backend = backend, .orm = .{ .backend = backend } };
    }
    pub fn thingRepo(self: *ThingPersistence) data.Repository(model.Thing) {
        return .{ .orm = &self.orm };
    }
};
```

### 4. service.zig — Business Logic
```zig
const zigmodu = @import("zigmodu");
const data = zigmodu.data;
const model = @import("model.zig");
const persistence = @import("persistence.zig");

pub const ThingService = struct {
    persistence: *persistence.ThingPersistence,
    pub fn init(p: *persistence.ThingPersistence) ThingService {
        return .{ .persistence = p };
    }
    // CRUD generated by zmodu; add custom methods in ext/service.zig
};
```

### 5. api.zig — REST Handlers
```zig
const http = @import("zigmodu").http;
const service = @import("service.zig");
const model = @import("model.zig");

pub const ThingApi = struct {
    service: *service.ThingService,
    pub fn init(svc: *service.ThingService) ThingApi { return .{ .service = svc }; }
    fn resolve(ctx: *http.Context) *ThingApi {
        return @ptrCast(@alignCast(ctx.user_data orelse unreachable));
    }

    pub fn registerRoutes(self: *ThingApi, group: *http.RouteGroup) !void {
        // Route params use {id} syntax (curly braces), NOT :id
        try group.get("/things", listThings, @ptrCast(@alignCast(self)));
        try group.get("/things/{id}", getThing, @ptrCast(@alignCast(self)));
        try group.post("/things", createThing, @ptrCast(@alignCast(self)));
        try group.put("/things/{id}", updateThing, @ptrCast(@alignCast(self)));
        try group.delete("/things/{id}", deleteThing, @ptrCast(@alignCast(self)));
    }

    fn listThings(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
        const s = resolve(ctx);
        const page = ctx.queryInt(usize, "page", 0);
        const size = ctx.queryInt(usize, "size", 10);
        const result = try s.service.listThings(page, size);
        try ctx.jsonStruct(200, result);
    }

    fn getThing(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
        const s = resolve(ctx);
        const id = try ctx.paramInt(i64, "id");
        if (try s.service.getThing(id)) |entity| {
            try ctx.jsonStruct(200, entity);
        } else { try ctx.json(404, "{\"error\":\"not found\"}"); }
    }

    fn createThing(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
        const s = resolve(ctx);
        const entity = ctx.bindJson(model.Thing) catch {
            try ctx.json(400, "{\"error\":\"invalid body\"}");
            return;
        };
        s.service.validateThing(entity) catch {
            try ctx.json(400, "{\"err\":\"validation failed\"}");
            return;
        };
        const created = try s.service.createThing(entity);
        try ctx.jsonStruct(201, created);
    }

    fn updateThing(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
        const s = resolve(ctx);
        const entity = ctx.bindJson(model.Thing) catch {
            try ctx.json(400, "{\"error\":\"invalid body\"}");
            return;
        };
        s.service.validateThing(entity) catch {
            try ctx.json(400, "{\"err\":\"validation failed\"}");
            return;
        };
        try s.service.updateThing(entity);
        try http.RenderExt.success(ctx, "ok");
    }

    fn deleteThing(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
        const s = resolve(ctx);
        const id = try ctx.paramInt(i64, "id");
        try s.service.deleteThing(id);
        try ctx.json(204, "");
    }
};
```

Key API patterns:
- Path params: `ctx.paramInt(T, "key")` / `ctx.paramStr("key")` — use `{key}` in route
- Query params: `ctx.queryInt(T, "key", default)` / `ctx.queryStr("key", default)`
- JSON body: `ctx.bindJson(T)` with `catch` for 400
- Responses: `ctx.jsonStruct(status, data)` for structs, `ctx.json(status, string)` for raw

### 6. ext/ — AI Extension Directory

**ext/service.zig** — custom business logic:
```zig
// ext/service.zig — survives zmodu regeneration
const std = @import("std");
const zigmodu = @import("zigmodu");
const thing_svc = @import("../service.zig");

pub const ThingServiceExt = struct {
    svc: *thing_svc.ThingService,
    backend: zigmodu.data.SqlxBackend,

    pub fn init(svc: *thing_svc.ThingService, backend: zigmodu.data.SqlxBackend) ThingServiceExt {
        return .{ .svc = svc, .backend = backend };
    }
    // Add custom business methods here
};
```

**ext/api.zig** — custom endpoints:
```zig
// ext/api.zig — survives zmodu regeneration
const std = @import("std");
const zigmodu = @import("zigmodu");
const ext_svc = @import("service.zig");  // same ext/ dir

pub const ThingApiExt = struct {
    ext: *ext_svc.ThingServiceExt,
    pub fn init(ext: *ext_svc.ThingServiceExt) ThingApiExt {
        return .{ .ext = ext };
    }
    pub fn registerRoutes(self: *ThingApiExt, group: *zigmodu.http.RouteGroup) !void {
        _ = self;
        // try group.get("/things/custom", myHandler, @ptrCast(@alignCast(self)));
    }
};
```

## Wiring into main.zig

```zig
// 1. Import — module.zig has barrel re-exports, no root.zig
const thing = @import("modules/thing/module.zig");

// 2. Persistence
var thing_p = thing.persistence.ThingPersistence.init(backend);

// 3. Service
var thing_svc = thing.service.ThingService.init(&thing_p);

// 4. API
var thing_api = thing.api.ThingApi.init(&thing_svc);

// 5. Routes
try thing_api.registerRoutes(&root);

// 6. Application — pass module directly (no .module suffix)
var app = try zigmodu.Application.init(init.io, allocator, "myapp", .{ thing, }, .{});
```

## Zig Reserved Words

Module names that are Zig keywords use `_mod` suffix:
- `error` → `error_mod`, `test` → `test_mod`, `return` → `return_mod`
